nodes.name: Rolling Stone Greatest Albums
nodes.description: Highlights from Rolling Stone magazine's definitive album rankings

items.prompt: Which 1971 Motown concept album about Vietnam, poverty, and ecology topped Rolling Stone's 2020 revision of the 500 Greatest Albums list?
items.explanation:
Marvin Gaye, 1971 — #1 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Concept album addressing Vietnam War, poverty, police brutality, and ecology
Motown founder Berry Gordy initially refused to release it, calling it uncommercial
Title track inspired by an incident of police brutality witnessed by Renaldo Benson of the Four Tops
First Motown album where an artist had full creative control
Debuted at #6 on the Billboard 200; all three singles reached the top 10
Recorded largely with the Funk Brothers, Motown's legendary house band
Was #6 on Rolling Stone's 2003 list before rising to #1 in 2020

items.prompt: Which 1966 album, largely conceived by one member of a California surf-rock group, directly inspired the Beatles to create 'Sgt. Pepper's'?
items.explanation:
The Beach Boys, 1966 — #2 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Brian Wilson conceived and produced the album while the rest of the band toured
Paul McCartney has cited it as the primary inspiration for Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band
Poor initial U.S. sales (peaked at #10); much bigger success in the UK where it reached #2
Featured session musicians from the Wrecking Crew rather than the full band on many tracks
'God Only Knows' — McCartney called it the greatest song ever written
'Wouldn't It Be Nice' and 'Caroline, No' were also singles, though neither reached #1
Wilson suffered a breakdown during sessions for the intended follow-up, Smile, which went unfinished until 2004

items.prompt: Which confessional 1971 singer-songwriter album — featuring 'River' and 'A Case of You' — is ranked #3 on Rolling Stone's 2020 greatest albums list?
items.explanation:
Joni Mitchell, 1971 — #3 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Widely considered the benchmark for confessional songwriting
Recorded mostly with just voice, piano, guitar, and Appalachian dulcimer
Songs reference relationships with Graham Nash ('My Old Man'), James Taylor ('This Flight Tonight'), and Leonard Cohen ('A Case of You')
'River' — opens with a minor-key riff on 'Jingle Bells' — became a modern holiday standard despite being a breakup song
Mitchell later said the album made her feel 'completely transparent' and she needed years to recover emotionally
Did not chart a hit single upon release; its reputation grew steadily over decades
Highest-ranked album by a solo female artist on the 2020 list

items.prompt: Which 1976 double album — featuring 'Sir Duke' and 'Isn't She Lovely' — was the culmination of its blind artist's extraordinary 1970s creative run?
items.answer: Songs in the Key of Life
alternates: Songs In the Key of Life
items.explanation:
Stevie Wonder, 1976 — #4 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Double album plus a bonus 4-song EP — 21 tracks total
Won Grammy Album of the Year at the 1977 ceremony
'Sir Duke' is a tribute to Duke Ellington; 'Isn't She Lovely' celebrates the birth of Wonder's daughter Aisha
Debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 — first album to do so by entering at the top in its first week
Capped Wonder's extraordinary 'classic period' of five albums in five years (1972–1976)
Over 130 musicians contributed to the sessions, which lasted two years
Wonder had threatened to leave Motown for a move to Ghana before signing a landmark $13 million contract
items.cardBack: Songs in the Key of Life

items.prompt: Which 1969 album — the last one its band recorded together, famous for a crosswalk cover photo — sits at #5 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list?
items.explanation:
The Beatles, 1969 — #5 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Last album the Beatles recorded together, though Let It Be was released later (1970)
Iconic cover photo shot on the zebra crossing outside EMI Studios on Abbey Road, St. John's Wood
Side two features a 16-minute medley stitching together song fragments — one of rock's great compositional feats
'Come Together' and 'Something' released as a double A-side single; 'Something' was the first Harrison-penned Beatles A-side
George Harrison's contributions ('Something,' 'Here Comes the Sun') are considered his finest Beatles work
The studio was renamed Abbey Road Studios in 1970 because of the album's fame
Paul McCartney appearing barefoot on the cover fueled the 'Paul is dead' conspiracy theory

items.prompt: Which 1991 grunge album's lead single dethroned Michael Jackson from #1 on the Billboard 200, marking the mainstream arrival of alternative rock?
items.explanation:
Nirvana, 1991 — #6 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Knocked Michael Jackson's Dangerous off #1 on the Billboard 200 in January 1992
'Smells Like Teen Spirit' became an anthem that effectively ended the dominance of hair metal
Produced by Butch Vig; mixed by Andy Wallace, whose polished sound bothered Kurt Cobain
Baby-in-the-pool cover shot cost $200; the baby (Spencer Elden) later sued the band
DKC Geffen expected to sell 50,000 copies; it sold 30 million worldwide
Cobain said he was 'trying to write the ultimate pop song' with 'Teen Spirit,' modeling it on the Pixies' loud-quiet-loud dynamic
Also contains 'Come as You Are,' 'Lithium,' and 'In Bloom'

items.prompt: Which 1977 album was infamously recorded while two couples within the band were breaking up, producing 'Dreams,' 'Go Your Own Way,' and 'The Chain'?
items.explanation:
Fleetwood Mac, 1977 — #7 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Recorded during simultaneous breakups: Lindsey Buckingham/Stevie Nicks and John/Christine McVie
Personal turmoil fueled confessional songwriting — 'Go Your Own Way' was Buckingham's breakup song to Nicks
'Dreams' was the band's only #1 single; went viral again in 2020 via a TikTok cranberry-juice skateboarding video
Won Grammy Album of the Year at the 1978 ceremony
Spent 31 weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200; approximately 40 million copies sold worldwide
'The Chain' — the only song credited to all five members — became the BBC's Formula 1 theme from 1978 onward
Recorded at Record Plant in Sausalito, CA amid heavy cocaine and alcohol use

items.prompt: Which 1984 semi-autobiographical film soundtrack spawned 'When Doves Cry' (famously recorded without a bass line) and 'Let's Go Crazy'?
items.explanation:
Prince and the Revolution, 1984 — #8 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Soundtrack to the semi-autobiographical 1984 film of the same name
'When Doves Cry' hit #1 on the Hot 100 for 5 weeks; famously has no bass line (Prince removed it last-minute)
Album, film, and single were all #1 simultaneously — only the third artist to achieve that triple
Spent 24 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200
'Let's Go Crazy,' the title track, and 'I Would Die 4 U' were also top-10 hits
Recorded partly at First Avenue nightclub in Minneapolis, which became a landmark because of the film
Won the Academy Award for Best Original Song Score

items.prompt: Which 1998 debut solo album by a former Fugees member became the first rap/hip-hop record to win the Grammy for Album of the Year?
items.explanation:
Lauryn Hill, 1998 — #10 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
First rap artist to win Grammy Album of the Year (41st ceremony, February 1999)
Won five Grammys total that night — the most by any artist at the ceremony
Debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 with ~423,000 first-week copies, a record for a female artist at the time
Hill was previously one-third of the Fugees, whose 1996 album The Score sold 22 million copies
'Doo Wop (That Thing)' debuted at #1 on the Hot 100 — the first hip-hop song by a female solo artist to do so
Title references the 1973 Marvin Gaye–influenced film The Education of Sonny Carson
Hill has never released a second solo studio album despite decades of anticipation

items.prompt: Which 1982 album — the best-selling record of all time at ~70 million copies — placed at #12 rather than #1 on Rolling Stone's 2020 greatest albums list?
items.explanation:
Michael Jackson, 1982 — #12 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Best-selling album of all time with estimated 70 million copies sold worldwide
Produced by Quincy Jones; yielded seven top-10 Billboard Hot 100 singles
'Billie Jean' music video broke MTV's de facto color barrier — the channel had rarely played Black artists
Won a record eight Grammy Awards at the 1984 ceremony
Title track's 14-minute music video, directed by John Landis, premiered as a theatrical event and transformed music videos into an art form
Spent 37 weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200
Was #20 on Rolling Stone's 2003 list; rose to #12 in 2020

items.prompt: Which 1979 double album by a British punk band spanned punk, reggae, rockabilly, and ska, and featured a bass-smashing cover photo inspired by Elvis Presley's debut?
items.explanation:
The Clash, 1979 — #16 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Double album sold at a single-album price — the band insisted to CBS Records
Spans punk, reggae, rockabilly, ska, R&B, and jazz in 19 tracks
Cover photo by Pennie Smith shows Paul Simonos smashing his bass onstage; layout mirrors Elvis Presley's debut album
Title track warns of apocalyptic flooding of the Thames — 'London calling to the faraway towns'
'Should I Stay or Should I Go' became a #1 UK hit in 1991 after use in a Levi's ad
'Train in Vain' was added last-minute and left off the back cover track listing; became their first U.S. top-30 hit
Produced by Guy Stevens, who reportedly threw chairs and ladders around the studio to create energy

items.prompt: Which 1967 concept album — once unanimously considered rock's greatest achievement — dropped from #1 on Rolling Stone's 2003 list to #24 in the 2020 revision?
items.answer: Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band
alternates: Sgt Peppers Lonely Hearts Club Band
Sgt Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band
items.explanation:
The Beatles, 1967 — #24 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list (was #1 in 2003 and 2012)
Widely credited as the first true 'concept album' in rock, though the concept is loosely maintained
Peter Blake's iconic cover collage features 70+ figures including Mae West, Karl Marx, and Edgar Allan Poe
Pioneered studio techniques: automatic double tracking, tape loops, a 40-piece orchestra on 'A Day in the Life'
The album took over 700 hours to record — unprecedented for the era; most albums took days
Won four Grammys including Album of the Year (the first rock album to do so)
'A Day in the Life' was banned by the BBC for alleged drug references ('I'd love to turn you on')
Its dramatic fall from #1 to #24 reflects the 2020 list's shift toward greater diversity of genre and era
items.cardBack: Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band

items.prompt: Which 1959 modal jazz album — the best-selling jazz record of all time — was largely improvised from sketches its trumpeter-bandleader brought to the session?
items.explanation:
Miles Davis, 1959 — #31 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Best-selling jazz album of all time with over 5 million copies sold in the U.S. alone
Pioneered modal jazz — improvisation based on scales rather than chord changes
Davis brought only skeletal sketches to the sessions; most takes were first or second attempts
Sextet included John Coltrane, Cannonball Adderley, Bill Evans (piano on most tracks), and Wynton Kelly
'So What' opens with a bass figure by Paul Chambers that became one of jazz's most recognizable riffs
Recorded in just two sessions at Columbia's 30th Street Studio in New York (March and April 1959)
Bill Evans's liner notes compared the music to Japanese sumi-e ink painting — spontaneous and unrepeatable

items.prompt: Which 1973 album — featuring 'Higher Ground' and 'Living for the City' — won Grammy Album of the Year for its blind Motown artist, three years before his double-album magnum opus?
items.explanation:
Stevie Wonder, 1973 — #34 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Won Grammy Album of the Year at the 1974 ceremony — the first of three consecutive AOTY wins for Wonder
'Living for the City' is a 7-minute narrative about a Black man migrating from the South to New York, with a cinematic spoken-word interlude
'Higher Ground' was recorded in a single 3-hour session; later famously covered by Red Hot Chili Peppers
Three days after the album's release, Wonder was in a serious car accident that left him in a coma for four days
Wonder played nearly every instrument on the album using overdubs and early synthesizers (ARP, Moog)
Part of his landmark 'classic period' (1972–1976) in which he released five critically acclaimed albums
Wonder had renegotiated his Motown contract at age 21, gaining full creative control — rare for any Motown artist

items.prompt: Which 1997 album by an Oxford band — featuring 'Paranoid Android' and 'Karma Police' — anticipated 21st-century anxieties about technology and alienation?
items.explanation:
Radiohead, 1997 — #42 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list
Dystopian themes of technological alienation, consumerism, and modern anxiety presaged the internet age
'Paranoid Android' is a 6.5-minute multi-part suite named after the character Marvin from The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
Title derived from the 'OK Computer' catchphrase in Douglas Adams's Hitchhiker's Guide
Recorded partly in actress Jane Seymour's 15th-century mansion (St. Catherine's Court) near Bath
'Karma Police' became the band's biggest UK single; its music video ends with a car engulfed in flames
Followed up by Kid A (2000), which abandoned guitars for electronics and ranked #20 on the same list
Nominated for Grammy Album of the Year but lost to Bob Dylan's Time Out of Mind
